metadata.notes: Drive times calculated from Surfers Paradise. Ferry required for Stradbroke Islands. 4WD required for Double Island Point.

gold_coast.name: Gold Coast
gold_coast.description: Snapper to Surfers Paradise. E-SE facing coastline. Picks up most swell directions well.
gold_coast.why_this_reference: D-Bah is the most consistent swell magnet on the Gold Coast. It faces ESE and picks up S, SE, and E swells. Its forecast data accurately represents what all Gold Coast south spots are receiving. Wind and tide data applies to the entire Coolangatta-to-Burleigh stretch.

tweed_coast.name: Tweed Coast (NSW)
tweed_coast.description: Fingal Head to Cabarita/Hastings Point. Similar swell window to Gold Coast but slightly more S exposure. Wind can differ.
tweed_coast.why_this_reference: Cabarita faces SE and is the most notable break on the Tweed Coast. It captures the S/SE swell window that differs from Gold Coast proper. Wind data here represents the Tweed coastal stretch from Fingal to Hastings Point.

byron_bay.name: Byron Bay
byron_bay.description: Cape Byron area. Unique E/NE facing orientation means very different swell window. Cape creates wind shadow effects.
byron_bay.why_this_reference: The Pass is the most monitored spot in Byron with detailed forecaster reports. Cape Byron's E/NE orientation is fundamentally different from the Gold Coast. The Pass forecast captures what E and NE swells are doing, and the wind data represents the Byron microclimate (which can be very different from GC on the same day).

ballina_lennox.name: Ballina / Lennox Head
ballina_lennox.description: Lennox to Ballina. SE-facing coastline, strong S swell exposure. Different wind patterns than Byron (no cape shelter).
ballina_lennox.why_this_reference: Lennox Head is the premier spot in this stretch and has detailed Surfline forecaster coverage. It faces SSE and picks up the S swells that define this region. Wind and tide data here covers the Lennox-Ballina-Skennars corridor accurately.

far_south.name: Evans Head / Yamba / Angourie
far_south.description: Southern extreme of range. E-SE facing. Significantly different wind and swell from Gold Coast. Only fetch if conditions look exceptional.
far_south.why_this_reference: Angourie is the world-class anchor spot for this region and has Surfline forecaster reports. It represents the southern swell window well. Evans Head, Yamba, and surrounding spots share similar swell exposure. This region is 2+ hours from Gold Coast so only worth checking when a big S/SE swell is running.

sunshine_coast.name: Sunshine Coast
sunshine_coast.description: Caloundra to Coolum. E-SE facing, more sheltered from S swells than Gold Coast. Different wind corridor.
sunshine_coast.why_this_reference: Mooloolaba is centrally located on the Sunshine Coast with good Surfline coverage. It represents the E/SE swell window that the Sunshine Coast receives. Wind data here is distinct from Gold Coast — the Sunshine Coast often gets different wind patterns due to the coastal geography north of Moreton Bay.

noosa.name: Noosa / Rainbow Beach
noosa.description: NE-facing points and beaches. Completely different swell window — needs E/NE swell. S swells barely register here.
noosa.why_this_reference: Noosa is THE reference for the NE-facing coastline. It has excellent Surfline forecaster reports and its NE orientation makes it a completely different swell window from anything south. When E/NE swells hit, Noosa can be pumping while the Gold Coast is flat, and vice versa. This is why a separate fetch is essential.

stradbroke_islands.name: Stradbroke Islands
stradbroke_islands.description: Offshore islands. E-SE exposure, but different wind and swell refraction due to island geography.
stradbroke_islands.why_this_reference: South Straddie has a Surfline page and represents the island swell window. The islands sit offshore and get unobstructed swell but different wind effects than the mainland. Ferry access means this region is only worth recommending on exceptional days.

metadata.fetch_strategy: Fetch the reference spot for each region. For the far_south and stradbroke_islands regions, only fetch if the general swell forecast suggests conditions worth the drive (big S/SE swell for far_south, clean E swell for stradbroke). The core 4 regions (gold_coast, tweed_coast, byron_bay, sunshine_coast/noosa) should always be fetched.

spots.name: Snapper Rocks
spots.wind_notes: Any S component works as offshore
spots.tide_notes: All tides workable depending on sand quality
spots.girlfriend_notes: Too crowded, too fast, too competitive
spots.character: Perfect barreling right-hander, freight-train speed, hollow tubes, 100m+ rides. The legendary Superbank connects to Kirra.

spots.name: Kirra
spots.girlfriend_notes: Too crowded and heavy
spots.character: Legendary tubes, fickle — doesn't break often but when it does, it's world-class. Sandbar dependent.

spots.name: Burleigh Heads
spots.girlfriend_notes: Heavy takeoff, steep, can be powerful and crowded
spots.character: Iconic freight-train barrel machine. Four sections rarely linking. Sand dependent from Tallebudgera River.

spots.name: Currumbin Alley
spots.girlfriend_notes: Fun walls, manageable size, not too heavy
spots.character: Quality walls and occasional barrels. Good angles from E/NE swells. Reliable and fun.

spots.name: Palm Beach
spots.girlfriend_notes: Beach break, manageable, good for cruisy sessions
spots.character: Exposed beach break with consistent surf. Multiple peaks along the beach.

spots.name: The Spit
spots.girlfriend_notes: Left-hander, accessible, but crowded
spots.character: Consistent exposed river break, left-hand wave, accessible for various skill levels.

spots.name: South Stradbroke Island
spots.girlfriend_notes: Uncrowded, mellow A-frames, sand bottom, forgiving
spots.character: Peak after peak of hollow A-frame perfection. 500m stretch of quality. Deep outside sandbars. Collects small S swells.

spots.name: Cylinder Beach
spots.girlfriend_notes: Gentle waves, sheltered, patrolled, ideal for mellow sessions
spots.character: Sheltered bay with gentle waves. Ideal learning spot. Patrolled year-round.

spots.name: North Stradbroke Main Beach
spots.girlfriend_notes: Heavy and hollow, powerful conditions
spots.character: Longest beach on Straddie (32km). Very heavy and hollow at times. Multiple peaks and sandbands.

spots.name: Point Lookout
spots.girlfriend_notes: Rocky, fast, hollow — not mellow enough
spots.character: Surfing mecca of Stradbroke. World-class breaks (South Gorge, Deadmans, Frenchmans). Fast hollow tubes, long peeling walls.

spots.name: Deadmans Beach
spots.girlfriend_notes: Less powerful than Main Beach, good alternative
spots.character: Popular alternative for locals. Open exposure but less powerful than Main Beach.

spots.name: Kings Beach
spots.girlfriend_notes: Fun, consistent, patrolled, family-friendly
spots.character: Fun and consistent beach breaks. Soft rolling waves. Highly patrolled, family-friendly.

spots.name: Moffat Beach
spots.girlfriend_notes: Fun right-hander, manageable, good vibe
spots.character: North-facing beach. Famous right-hand point break. Australia's longest running surf comp venue (Pa & Ma Bendall).

spots.name: Dicky Beach
spots.girlfriend_notes: Quieter than Kings Beach, fun beach break
spots.character: Combination of fun beach breaks and powerful waves. Quieter than Kings Beach, mainly locals.
hazards: can get powerful in larger swells

spots.name: Maroochydore
spots.girlfriend_notes: Soft, beginner-friendly, mellow rivermouth left
spots.character: Cool rivermouth left off Pincushion Island sandbar. Softer beaches. Perfect for beginners.

spots.name: Mooloolaba
spots.girlfriend_notes: Mellow, sheltered, sandy, very forgiving
spots.character: Mellow beach break with left & right-handers. Protected by Point Cartwright. Sheltered and manageable.

spots.name: Alexandra Headland
spots.girlfriend_notes: Cruisy longboard wave, sand-bottom peelers, mellow
spots.character: Mellow right reef with sand-bottom peelers. Cruisy longboard wave. Year-round lifeguards.

spots.name: Coolum Beach
spots.girlfriend_notes: Multiple peaks, consistent, good for all levels
spots.character: Exposed beach and reef break with consistent surf. Groundswell dominant. Open beach with multiple peaks.

spots.name: Peregian Beach
spots.girlfriend_notes: Fun, playful, less crowded than nearby tourist spots
spots.character: Open beach break with plenty of swell exposure. Fun and playful. Less crowded than nearby tourist spots.
hazards: can get overpowering in big swells

spots.name: Marcus Beach / Castaways
spots.girlfriend_notes: Uncrowded, consistent, relaxed vibe
spots.character: Consistent good quality waves. Easy access. Popular with locals. Less crowded alternative.

spots.name: Noosa First Point
spots.girlfriend_notes: Perfect peeling rights, mellow, longboard heaven — but very crowded
spots.character: Premier QLD longboard wave. Perfectly peeling right-handers. 100m+ rides. World-famous longboarding mecca.

spots.name: Noosa Tea Tree Bay
spots.girlfriend_notes: Quality walls, longer rides than Granite Bay, needs a bit more swell
spots.character: Jewel in Noosa's crown. Outside reef section with moving takeoff. World-class right-hander with good barrels.

spots.name: Noosa Granite Bay
spots.girlfriend_notes: Mellow, well-suited for longboarders, marching rows of rights
spots.character: Easternmost of major Noosa points. Fairy Pools reef cluster. Marching rows of rights. Mellow and well-suited for longboarders.

spots.name: Sunshine Beach
spots.girlfriend_notes: Peaky beach break, more size than points — good when chest high and clean
spots.character: Peaky open beach break with more size than the point breaks. Consistent winter option.

spots.name: Double Island Point
spots.drive_notes: 4WD required from Rainbow Beach via beach drive. Check tide times — high tide impassable.
spots.wind_notes: SE to E — unusual, onshores are actually offshore here
spots.tide_notes: All tides work, but low tide needed for beach access drive
spots.girlfriend_notes: 300m+ rides, mellow even when small, uncrowded paradise — but requires 4WD commitment
spots.character: Australia's longest and safest beginner wave. 300m+ rides in ideal conditions. Remote in Great Sandy National Park. Perfect for longboarders.

spots.name: Rainbow Beach
spots.tide_notes: Low tide for beach driving access
spots.girlfriend_notes: Mellow, uncrowded, scenic — adventure day trip
spots.character: Gateway to Double Island Point. Sand point breaks. Mellow beginner-friendly. Scenic coloured sand cliffs.

spots.name: Duranbah (D-Bah)
spots.girlfriend_notes: Works on all sizes, punchy A-frames, fun on small days — but can be crowded
spots.character: Punchy A-frame wedges. Barrel opportunities. Reliable swell magnet. HPC training spot.

spots.name: Fingal Head
spots.girlfriend_notes: Uncrowded, calm, but fickle and inconsistent
spots.character: Exposed beach with headland. Fickle and inconsistent. Sandbar-dependent quality.

spots.name: Cabarita Beach
spots.girlfriend_notes: Long sectioned right, manageable when clean, not too crowded
spots.character: Long sectioned right-hander. Highly sand-dependent. Can resemble Burleigh on good days. Fast and hollow sections.

spots.name: Hastings Point
spots.girlfriend_notes: Uncrowded, rivermouth break, good even on smaller days
spots.character: Rivermouth break. Good even on smaller days. Uncrowded alternative. Deep nearshore gutters create quality peaks.

spots.name: Kingscliff
spots.tide_notes: Mid to high for reef section
spots.girlfriend_notes: Fun beach break peaks, mellow, beginner-friendly, not crowded
spots.character: Fun beach break peaks. Mellow waves. Beginner-friendly. Year-round possibility.

spots.name: Casuarina Beach
spots.girlfriend_notes: Very uncrowded, consistent, good for learning/cruising
spots.character: Exposed sandbar. Fairly consistent. Left and right waves. Great for avoiding crowds.

spots.name: Salt Beach
spots.girlfriend_notes: Quiet alternative to Kingscliff, accessible
spots.character: Alternative to main Kingscliff. Quieter option. Accessible beach break.

spots.name: Brunswick Heads
spots.girlfriend_notes: Fun on smaller days, but needs decent swell to work well
spots.character: Long arc of golden sand. Refracted sets in SE channel. Sandbars. Winter-focused.

spots.name: The Pass (Byron Bay)
spots.girlfriend_notes: Long peeling rights, mellow on small days — but insanely crowded
spots.character: Iconic long right-hand point. Long peeling walls. Occasional hollow sections. Extended rides.

spots.name: Wategos Beach
spots.girlfriend_notes: Long slow fat waves, perfect for longboards, gentle rolling breaks
spots.character: Long, slow, fat waves. Perfect for longboards. Gentle rolling breaks. Sheltered by Cape Byron.
hazards: can get full at high tide

spots.name: Tallows Beach
spots.girlfriend_notes: Powerful beach break, strong rips, not beginner-friendly
spots.character: Long clean sand beach. Consistent winter breaks. Powerful beach break. Exposed to everything S-NE. 'Cosy Corner' for best wedges.

spots.name: The Wreck (Belongil)
spots.girlfriend_notes: Fast, hollow, powerful — technical takeoff
spots.character: Fast, hollow, powerful right-hander on sand in front of shipwreck. Sheltered. Technical take-off.

spots.name: Broken Head
spots.girlfriend_notes: Sand-dependent quality, occasionally hollow — inconsistent for mellow sessions
spots.character: Real gem of Byron Bay. Occasionally hollow. Sand-dependent. Room to wander beach if point isn't on.

spots.name: Lennox Head Point
spots.girlfriend_notes: World-class point but heavy, rocky, crowded, needs bigger swell
spots.character: World-class right point. Quality long green walls. Hollow sections. Rocky reef base. 0.8 mile long.

spots.name: Lennox Head Beach (Seven Mile)
spots.girlfriend_notes: Gentle beach breaks, less crowded alternative to the point
spots.character: Wide unprotected sandy beach. Gentle beach breaks. Surf school frequent. Alternative to crowded point.

spots.name: Skennars Head
spots.girlfriend_notes: Reef break, needs decent size, not mellow
spots.character: Reef break in small bay. Exposed to SE swells. A-frame beach breaks nearby. Less crowded than Lennox.

spots.name: Shelly Beach (Ballina)
spots.tide_notes: Left off north rocks, right off south rocks
spots.girlfriend_notes: Gentle waves, soft sand, family-friendly
spots.character: Gentle waves ideal for beginners/kids. Soft white sand. Gentle slope. Family-friendly.

spots.name: Lighthouse Beach (Ballina)
spots.girlfriend_notes: Northern end gentle, good intermediate playground
spots.character: Reliable surf breaks. Northern end gentle, southern end challenging near North Wall.

spots.name: Sharpes Beach (Ballina)
spots.girlfriend_notes: Family-friendly, uncrowded, good beginner option
spots.character: Well-loved by locals. Patrolled in school holidays. Family-friendly. Good beginner option.

spots.name: Flat Rock (Ballina)
spots.girlfriend_notes: Fun left and right waves — but known for sharks
spots.character: Rocky outcrop creates break. Left and right waves. Mix of rock ledge and sand bottom. Consistent.

spots.name: Evans Head Main Beach
spots.girlfriend_notes: Protected, beginner-friendly, uncrowded quiet town
spots.character: Protected from big SE swells. Right and left fun beach breaks. Beginner-friendly. Only patrolled beach in Evans Head.

spots.name: Chinamans Beach (Evans Head)
spots.girlfriend_notes: Exposed, dangerous in easterly swell, for more experienced surfers
spots.character: Serene escape. Bigger than Main Beach on small days. Particularly dangerous in easterly swell (exposed).

spots.name: Turners Beach (Yamba)
spots.girlfriend_notes: Soft manageable beach break, slow-peeling, great for longboarders, uncrowded
spots.character: Soft, manageable beach break. Slow-peeling waves. Great intermediate playground. Perfect for longboarders/beginners.

spots.name: Pippi Beach (Yamba)
spots.girlfriend_notes: Bowly shorebreak, can be heavy
spots.character: Throws bowly shorebreaks. Works best with N winds. Exposed to SE/NE swell channels.

spots.name: Angourie Point
spots.drive_notes: At the edge of 2hr radius — only go if conditions are firing
spots.girlfriend_notes: World-class but heavy, crowded, rocky, needs decent size
spots.character: One of world's best point breaks. SE produces fatter sets, E-NE more hollow/barreling. Incredibly versatile.

spots.name: Angourie Back Beach
spots.girlfriend_notes: Sheltered, uncrowded, great for beginners/longboarders
spots.character: Sheltered from northerly winds. Local favourite. Great for swimming and beginner surfers/longboarders. Uncrowded.

spots.name: Spookys (Angourie)
spots.girlfriend_notes: Small swell = knee-high learner waves; big swell = expert only. Two faces.
spots.character: Two faces — small swell: knee-high learner waves. Big swell: cobblestone reef ferocious A-frames 50m off rocks.
